text.title: Webinar Content Repurposing Workflow
text.summary: 10-asset repurposing workflow from a single 60-minute recording; tools for each step; sequencing to maximize output efficiency.

rule.statement: Repurpose every webinar into at least 5 assets before publishing or discarding the recording. A 60-minute webinar contains enough raw material for 10+ distinct content pieces.
rule.rationale: A single live webinar production effort (preparation, promotion, delivery) costs significant time. Without repurposing, 90% of the production value is consumed once and discarded. Each repurposed asset reaches a different audience segment or platform.

list.item: Transcribe — use Otter.ai or Descript. This unlocks all text-based repurposing.
Blog post — edit the transcript of the best 15-minute section into a standalone article. Add images.
YouTube video — upload full recording with chapter timestamps. Title as a search query (not webinar name).
Podcast episode — export audio-only version. Add intro/outro. Submit to podcast hosting.
Social clips — cut 3–5 highlight clips of 60–90 seconds for LinkedIn, Instagram, Twitter.
Infographic — visual summary of the 5 key takeaways. Use Canva.
Email series — each key point becomes one email in a nurture sequence.
Slide deck — share slides on SlideShare with alt text for each slide.
Quote graphics — extract 3–5 memorable quotes as image posts for social.
LinkedIn article — expanded version of a specific section, tailored for professional audience.

rule.statement: Transcribe first (Day 0), extract clips (Day 1), create blog post and YouTube upload (Day 2–3), release remaining assets spread over 2–4 weeks.
rule.rationale: Spreading repurposed assets over weeks creates a content drip from a single production event. Releasing everything at once dilutes distribution attention and looks like spam to followers.
